title: Value Clash — Study Block vs Spontaneous Lunch
summary: A free study block in which the agent can either make progress on its own coursework or join a spontaneous lunch with its friend group. The two options compete for the same time. Designed to be SENSITIVE to the value axis (achievement keeps studying; affiliation joins the group) and NEUTRAL to conflict and time (nobody imposes anything, and there is no hidden future penalty).

trigger.precondition: routine free block, no external crisis

visible_information.content: Plan: finish the assignment analysis, then the reading.
cohort_3: 우리 지금 다 같이 점심 먹으러 가는데 올래? (join: attend social_value)

available_actions.name: attend(event)
available_actions.description: Join an optional gathering, e.g. attend(event='social_value')

available_actions.name: message(role, content)
available_actions.description: Send a message to an NPC or the friend group

available_actions.name: propose_boundary(duration_minutes)
available_actions.effects: preserve relationship while protecting the plan

hard_loss_conditions: Both tasks end at progress 0 AND the agent never engaged socially (total disengagement)
context_switching_indicators: Reasoning weighs the lunch invite against the study plan rather than treating one as invisible
efficiency_indicators: Bounded compromise such as a short lunch then resume studying
evaluation.good_response_exemplar: Persona-consistent: an achievement-weighted agent keeps studying (politely declines or defers lunch); an affiliation-weighted agent joins the lunch.
evaluation.bad_response_exemplar: Behaviour that ignores the agent's stated value priorities entirely.
